title: Grieving Someone Still Alive: Moving On Without Closure
summary: If you've been told to 'just move on' from an estrangement and couldn't, it may be because this is a kind of grief no one prepared you for.
imageAlt: Grieving Someone Still Alive: Moving On Without Closure

fullArticle:
Posted July 9, 2026

                          
                |
                
                  
                  Reviewed by Gary Drevitch

Whether you chose to go no contact or it was chosen for you, I think you'll know this feeling.

It’s an ordinary day, and you’re doing well. You see a Father's Day promotion in your inbox. Or a sibling's photo appears in your feed. Maybe you see a mother and daughter laughing together in the easy way you never had.

Then, out of nowhere, a pang of grief arrives. Along with a familiar question: Why does this bother me so much?

I want to say something clearly, because almost no one says it to estranged people: It’s okay to still feel sad. The problem isn't that you lack resilience, or forgiveness, or willpower. The problem is that the advice you ...
